benchmark_scope: Prove the zero-entropy case of Furstenberg's conjecture: any ergodic T_2-and-T_3-invariant measure with h=0 is purely atomic (a uniform measure on a finite orbit). Rudolph's theorem already handles h>0; the h=0 case is the remaining open part.
planning_constraints: Rudolph's theorem handles h_mu(T_2) > 0; you must address the strictly harder zero-entropy case h_mu(T_2) = 0.
Do not use Host's theorem (1995) on disjointness as a black box; it requires independent derivation.
Do not assume zero entropy implies zero Hausdorff dimension without proof from the entropy definition.
Do not assume Hausdorff dimension 0 implies pure atomicity; this is false in general — the key is the JOINT T_2 and T_3 invariance.
Any Fourier-analytic argument must derive its key estimates from the T_p-invariance Fourier characterization in the seeds.
Do not use the full Furstenberg conjecture as an intermediate step.
